notes: Initial smoke flagged 2 PDFs where page 1 renders as blank. Investigation showed these have decorative front covers / blank versos by design — page 2 of each has real content (3.7% and 2.5% non-white respectively).
The pipeline's correct behaviour is to render extra pages when a blank is detected, NOT to blocklist the PDF. See `pypdfium2_blank_handling` below.
Result: 944/944 PDFs are renderable. Blocklist is empty in v1.

front_cover_blank.reason: page 1 is blank front cover, page 2+ has content (3.7% non-white)
page 1 is blank front cover, page 2+ has content (2.5% non-white)

pypdfium2_blank_handling.rule: When the rasterizer requests N pages for a chunk, detect blanks (heuristic: <0.1% non-white pixels in grayscale, configurable) and render extra pages to fill the chunk to N non-blank pages. Cap extra at 1.5 × requested to avoid runaway on PDFs with many blank versos.
pypdfium2_blank_handling.applies_to: metadata pass (always page 1 chunk), summarization pass (continuation chunks)
